1_TX_DataCenters.bridge_power_type: BTM gas (GE Vernova LM2500XPRESS aeroderivative turbines) + grid
1_TX_DataCenters.notes: FLAGSHIP Stargate site. Ph1 operational Sep 30 2025 = EXEMPT from SB-6 curtailment. 1,100-acre campus. On-site gas generators + Oncor grid. Ph2: 6 more bldgs targeting mid-2026 = SUBJECT to SB-6. Oracle leases for OpenAI. 4M sqft full build. Capped at 1.2GW Mar 2026 due to grid delay. Source: Crusoe press release Sep 2025; DCD Feb 2026; WinBuzzer Mar 2026

1_TX_DataCenters.sb6_curtailment_obligation: MANDATORY — curtailment protocols required; remote disconnect install required pre-energization
1_TX_DataCenters.sb6_backup_gen_disclosure: YES — must disclose if on-site gen can serve ≥50% of demand
1_TX_DataCenters.bridge_power_type: BTM gas (existing turbines from Ph1) + planned solar+BESS
1_TX_DataCenters.notes: Ph2 energizing mid-2026 = FULLY under SB-6. Capped at 1.2GW total campus due to Oncor grid delay >1yr. Power currently from on-site GE Vernova aeroderivative gas turbines AND Oncor grid. Crusoe/Oracle/OpenAI capped expansion. Meta in talks for vacant space. Source: DCD Feb 2026; WinBuzzer Mar 2026

1_TX_DataCenters.sb6_curtailment_obligation: MANDATORY — curtailment protocols; remote disconnect required
1_TX_DataCenters.sb6_backup_gen_disclosure: YES — 700MW natural gas microgrid = >50% of demand; must disclose
1_TX_DataCenters.bridge_power_type: Off-grid BTM natural gas microgrid (700MW Voltagrid generators) — bypasses ERCOT grid entirely for initial ops
1_TX_DataCenters.notes: 40min from Abilene Stargate. 10 bldgs 3.7M sqft $25B. 700MW Voltagrid natural gas microgrid — BYPASSES grid entirely for initial ops. Oracle tenant (Stargate). SB-6 still applies when/if grid-connected load exceeds 75MW. Net metering review required if co-locating with existing generator. Source: Business Insider; Blackridge; DCD Aug 2025

1_TX_DataCenters.developer: Google / Intersect Power / TPG Rise Climate
1_TX_DataCenters.sb6_backup_gen_disclosure: YES — co-located solar+BESS; must disclose BTM generation
1_TX_DataCenters.bridge_power_type: BTM solar + BESS collocated (Google/Intersect partnership — FIRST industrial park model in Texas)
1_TX_DataCenters.notes: DEMO HERO PROJECT: One of two Haskell County DCs EXPLICITLY paired with solar+BESS plant. Google $40B TX investment announced Nov 14 2025. First Google/Intersect Power/TPG Rise Climate industrial park. Co-location designed to 'reduce need for new infrastructure.' GHI in Haskell: ~5.7 kWh/m2/day. Source: Google/InnovationMap Nov 2025; Google TX data center page

1_TX_DataCenters.bridge_power_type: Grid-connected; PPAs with AES/Enel/Clearway/ENGIE/SB Energy/Orsted/X-Elio (6,200MW contracted)
1_TX_DataCenters.notes: Second Haskell County campus. No solar colocation announced for this one. Google 6,200MW PPA portfolio for TX grid. Air cooling (no evaporative). Source: Google TX DC page; Texas Tribune Nov 2025

1_TX_DataCenters.bridge_power_type: Grid-connected; Texas Panhandle wind+solar PPA portfolio
1_TX_DataCenters.notes: Armstrong County in Texas Panhandle. Highest GHI in TX: ~6.1 kWh/m2/day. High wind resource too. Xcel Energy SPS territory (not ERCOT — NOTE: SPS is SPP interconnection, not ERCOT; SB-6 may not apply if in SPP territory). Verify TSP. Source: Texas Tribune; Google TX page Feb 2026

1_TX_DataCenters.phase: Ph1 500MW tranche approved ERCOT; first 200MW May 2026
1_TX_DataCenters.sb6_curtailment_obligation: MANDATORY — first 200MW energizing May 2026 is first major >75MW load under SB-6
1_TX_DataCenters.sb6_backup_gen_disclosure: YES — 1.8GW switchyard on-site; backup gen status TBD
1_TX_DataCenters.bridge_power_type: Grid-connected (1.8GW switchyard planned); BTM solar viable on 768 acres
1_TX_DataCenters.notes: 768 acres between Dallas and Fort Worth. 1.8GW switchyard planned. 24 bldgs 3 phases. First 500MW ERCOT-approved. First 200MW energizing May 2026 = SUBJECT to SB-6. This is the highest-profile DFW project under SB-6. Source: Blackridge; datacenter construction reports 2025-2026
$1.5B+ investment. 2015 groundbreak. Operational. Pre-existing large load = EXEMPT from SB-6 new curtailment requirements. Source: Meta data centers page

$1.5B+ investment. 2025 groundbreak. El Paso Electric territory (WECC, not ERCOT). NOTE: El Paso Electric is WECC not ERCOT — SB-6 does NOT apply (SB-6 is ERCOT-specific). Verify interconnection territory before applying SB-6 flag. Source: Meta DC page; El Paso Times Dec 2024

1_TX_DataCenters.bridge_power_type: Grid via Bluebonnet EMC; BTM solar viable on 1,515 acres
1_TX_DataCenters.notes: 1,515 acres between Austin and San Antonio. 18-month utility engagement. Bluebonnet Electric Cooperative (EMC — not ERCOT TSP directly). 360MW initial target 2028. Full 2GW long-term. NOTE: Co-op territory = similar regulatory dynamic to DC BLOX in Georgia. SB-6 applies to co-ops too per §39.170. Source: Blackridge; press releases May 2025

1_TX_DataCenters.sb6_applies: CHECK — CPS Energy is municipal utility
1_TX_DataCenters.sb6_curtailment_obligation: CPS Energy is municipally-owned utility (MOE) — SB-6 curtailment protocols APPLY to MOEs per §39.170
1_TX_DataCenters.notes: 200MW campus on West Military Dr & Loop 1604 SA. On-site 334MW CPS Energy substation. 5 bldgs 1.5M sqft $400M. CPS Energy is San Antonio's municipal utility — SB-6 explicitly extends curtailment to MOEs (§39.170). Source: Blackridge; Stream press releases

1_TX_DataCenters.sb6_curtailment_obligation: SPP territory: SB-6 is ERCOT-specific — may NOT apply. Federal FERC rules apply instead.
1_TX_DataCenters.bridge_power_type: BTM natural gas + planned nuclear (4x Westinghouse AP1000 reactors by 2032)
1_TX_DataCenters.notes: 5,800 acres near Pantex DOE facility. 11GW total vision. Ph1: 530MW initial, 1GW by late 2026. Near major gas pipelines. 4 nuclear reactors planned (2032+). Ex-Gov Rick Perry involved. Xcel SPS territory = SPP not ERCOT. SB-6 does NOT apply — federal FERC/SPP rules instead. Source: Texas Tech/Fermi Jun 2025; Reuters Jun 2025

1_TX_DataCenters.bridge_power_type: BTM BESS (31MW/62MWh LFP) — sized to jump interconnection queue years early
1_TX_DataCenters.notes: REFERENCE PRECEDENT for demo. Nation's first BTM BESS specifically sized to accelerate interconnection. Calibrant Energy system. Grid-responsive asset 'shrinks' apparent load. Announced Oct 22 2025. Blueprint for Texas SB-6 compliance via BESS. Source: pv magazine USA Oct 2025; Latitude Media Nov 2025

2_TX_PowerInfrastructure.operator: SL Energy Power Plant I LLC

Representative of ERCOT BESS deployment — 179GW BESS in ERCOT queue per Jan 2026

3_TX_BTM_Siting.sb6_bess_compliance_value: DIRECT — BESS reduces net grid draw; helps avoid SB-6 curtailment trigger; 4CP avoidance value $40-45K/MW/yr; reduces apparent load = smaller Oncor transmission upgrade requirement
3_TX_BTM_Siting.permitting_pathway: PUCT approval needed for net metering with existing BTM gas gen (§39.169); new BTM solar <75MW net: simpler path; larger: full ERCOT LLI review
3_TX_BTM_Siting.notes: Crusoe already has BTM gas turbines on-site. Solar+BESS layer is additive. 1,100-acre campus — 15% = 165 acres available for BTM solar = 825MW potential. GHI 5.70 excellent. 4CP charge reduction at $40-45K/MW/yr is ~$33M/yr for 825MW. Oncor transmission constraint is the binding constraint — BESS reduces apparent load. Source: NREL NSRDB; CPower 4CP benchmarks

3_TX_BTM_Siting.sb6_bess_compliance_value: CRITICAL — Project uses 700MW off-grid natural gas microgrid. When/if grid-connected, BESS enables load-shaping to minimize SB-6 curtailment exposure. 4CP avoidance value significant for any grid-connected portion.
3_TX_BTM_Siting.permitting_pathway: Off-grid BTM gas = no ERCOT grid interconnection = SB-6 curtailment does not apply to non-grid load per §39.170; BUT when grid backup needed, any >75MW grid draw triggers SB-6
3_TX_BTM_Siting.estimated_deploy_months: 18-24 (large scale; remote county; but ample land)
3_TX_BTM_Siting.notes: 700MW Voltagrid natural gas microgrid means Project Frontier bypasses ERCOT grid initially. Solar+BESS on 1,200 acres would reduce gas runtime costs and provide cleaner daytime power. GHI 5.80 — best in cluster. 1,200 acres × 15% = 180 acres = 900MW solar potential. Enormous resource. Source: Business Insider; NREL NSRDB

3_TX_BTM_Siting.sb6_bess_compliance_value: BENCHMARK — Google explicitly chose solar+BESS colocation to 'reduce need for new infrastructure.' This is the SB-6 compliance model for Texas. BESS discharges during 4CP events; solar covers daytime load; reduces Oncor transmission upgrade cost; qualifies for §37.0561 BTM exemption pathway.
3_TX_BTM_Siting.permitting_pathway: Google/Intersect Power industrial park = first-of-kind in Texas. Net metering arrangement with new generator (Intersect solar+BESS) = new colocation, §39.169 review NOT required (new generator post Sep 1 2025 = no pre-existing generator review). Streamlined path.
3_TX_BTM_Siting.notes: DEMO HERO. Google is building the EXACT model this demo argues for. One Haskell DC explicitly collocated with Intersect Power solar+BESS. GHI 5.72 excellent. No disclosed MW but implied large campus. This project is the proof the argument works at scale. Source: Google/InnovationMap Nov 2025; Google TX DC page

3_TX_BTM_Siting.sb6_bess_compliance_value: HIGH VALUE — GHI 6.10 is among best in continental US. Wind resource also exceptional (Panhandle). BUT Armstrong County is Xcel SPS territory = SPP not ERCOT. SB-6 does NOT apply. Federal FERC rules apply. Solar+BESS still valuable for load management and renewable commitments.
3_TX_BTM_Siting.permitting_pathway: Xcel SPS territory: TX Commission on Environmental Quality air permit for BTM gen; no ERCOT LLI; SPP interconnection rules apply instead
3_TX_BTM_Siting.estimated_deploy_months: 12-18 (Xcel SPS has faster large load process than Oncor currently)
3_TX_BTM_Siting.notes: BEST SOLAR RESOURCE in the dataset. 6.10 GHI = ~31% capacity factor. Panhandle wind also exceptional. Google's $40B TX plan includes 6,200MW PPA contracts — Armstrong County is closest to where those wind/solar resources are. SB-6 exclusion is important caveat. Source: NREL NSRDB Panhandle data; Texas Tribune Nov 2025

3_TX_BTM_Siting.sb6_bess_compliance_value: SB-6 COMPLIANCE DRIVER — First major DFW project energizing under SB-6 (May 2026). BESS enables: (1) Load shaping to avoid 4CP peaks ($40-45K/MW/yr savings), (2) Reduce apparent 75MW+ grid draw below threshold OR meet curtailment via BESS discharge, (3) SB-6 §37.0561 BTM pathway if BESS paired with on-site gen.
3_TX_BTM_Siting.permitting_pathway: Oncor territory; ERCOT LLI required for 200MW+; new BTM solar via standard construction permits; BESS colocation review under §39.169 if using existing generator; new BESS + new solar = clean path
3_TX_BTM_Siting.estimated_deploy_months: 12-18 (urban/suburban DFW — solar constrained by land density but 768 acres available)
3_TX_BTM_Siting.notes: 768 acres in DFW Metroplex. GHI 5.10 = moderate (lower than West TX). 768 × 15% = 115 acres = 575MW solar potential. First 200MW under SB-6 in May 2026. 4CP reduction on 200MW = $8-9M/yr in transmission charge savings. This is the flagship DFW SB-6 compliance story. Source: NREL NSRDB DFW data; Blackridge

3_TX_BTM_Siting.sb6_bess_compliance_value: CO-OP COMPLEXITY — Similar to DC BLOX/Greystone in Georgia. Bluebonnet EMC territory. SB-6 applies to co-ops (§39.170 explicitly extends to 'electric cooperatives'). But co-op interconnection process different from Oncor. BESS still reduces 4CP exposure and SB-6 curtailment risk.
3_TX_BTM_Siting.permitting_pathway: Bluebonnet EMC territory: co-op rules; SB-6 curtailment applies; EMC must develop curtailment protocols per §39.170; BTM solar via standard TX construction permit; net metering arrangement needs ERCOT/PUCT review if using existing EMC generation
3_TX_BTM_Siting.estimated_deploy_months: 18-30 (co-op process slower; 138kV may need upgrade for 360MW+)
3_TX_BTM_Siting.notes: 1,515 acres = enormous solar potential. GHI 5.30 = good (Austin climate). 1,515 × 15% = 227 acres = 1,135MW solar potential vs 360MW initial load = 315% coverage ratio. But 138kV substation constrains large load — transmission upgrade needed for full campus. BTM solar behind the meter on-site avoids this for on-site generation. Source: NREL NSRDB Austin area; Blackridge May 2025

4_SB6_Economics.curtailment_risk_without_bess: HIGH: 1GW draw without BESS = full SB-6 curtailment exposure during ERCOT emergencies; Oncor grid constraint already forced 1.2GW cap
4_SB6_Economics.sb6_compliance_pathway: BESS sized to cover 200MW peak demand during 4CP events; discharge during 4 monthly 15-min peaks Jun-Sep; solar covers 82.5% of daytime IT load; combined eliminates most curtailment risk
4_SB6_Economics.notes_economics: BESS 200MW/800MWh at $250/kWh = $200M. BTM solar 825MW (15% of 1,100 acres) at $0.95M/MW = $784M. Total BTM = $984M. vs Gas 300MW bridge at $1.5M/MW = $450M. But solar+BESS covers 82.5% of daytime load permanently vs gas = temporary bridge. 4CP savings: 200MW × $42,500 = $8.5M/yr. Payback on BESS alone from 4CP: ~24 yrs. But SB-6 compliance value + avoided gas costs + avoided curtailment uptime risk = compelling. Source: NREL TP-6A40-93281; CPower 4CP benchmark

4_SB6_Economics.sb6_applies: YES — when grid-connected; BTM gas currently bypasses ERCOT
4_SB6_Economics.curtailment_risk_without_bess: MEDIUM: 700MW off-grid gas currently exempt from SB-6 grid curtailment. When/if grid backup used >75MW, SB-6 applies. Long-term grid transition creates compliance need.
4_SB6_Economics.sb6_compliance_pathway: BTM gas (700MW Voltagrid) currently provides primary power off-grid. Solar+BESS would reduce gas runtime, cut fuel costs, and provide SB-6 compliance if/when grid-connected. No gas bridge needed — gas already in place.
4_SB6_Economics.notes_economics: Solar 900MW (15% of 1,200 acres) at $0.95M/MW = $855M. BESS 300MW/1,200MWh at $250/kWh = $300M. Total $1.155B. But replaces gas fuel costs (~$0.04-0.06/kWh for peaker gas). At 900MW solar × 30% CF × 8760hrs = 2.36TWh/yr; at $50/MWh avoided gas cost = $118M/yr fuel savings. Payback ~10 years on fuel savings alone. Source: NREL; EIA gas price benchmarks

4_SB6_Economics.sb6_applies: YES — MANDATORY; Google already building colocation model
4_SB6_Economics.curtailment_risk_without_bess: LOW: Google proactively collocating solar+BESS = the correct SB-6 strategy. Minimal curtailment risk by design.
4_SB6_Economics.sb6_compliance_pathway: Google is already building the model. Intersect Power solar+BESS colocation. SB-6 compliance built-in by design. No gas bridge needed. Sets the benchmark for other Texas projects.
4_SB6_Economics.notes_economics: Google's Haskell DC1 is the PROOF CASE for this demo. Solar+BESS colocation explicitly announced Nov 2025. 6,200MW PPA portfolio for TX grid. GHI 5.72. Intersect Power/TPG Rise Climate partnership. New colocation = no §39.169 review required (new generator). Fast-track path. Source: Google/InnovationMap Nov 2025

4_SB6_Economics.sb6_applies: YES — MANDATORY (first major DFW project under SB-6, May 2026)
4_SB6_Economics.curtailment_risk_without_bess: HIGH: First 200MW under SB-6 in DFW. No BTM generation announced. Full curtailment exposure during ERCOT emergencies. 4CP charges are significant in Oncor territory.
4_SB6_Economics.sb6_compliance_pathway: BESS 50MW/200MWh sized to cover 4CP events on 200MW load; solar 575MW on 768 acres covers 287% of daytime IT load. DFW GHI 5.10 = moderate. 4CP savings: 50MW × $42,500 = $2.1M/yr. This is the first DFW project that MUST comply with SB-6 and has no disclosed BTM strategy.
4_SB6_Economics.notes_economics: 50MW BESS at $250/kWh × 200MWh = $50M. 575MW solar at $0.95M/MW = $546M. Total $596M. DFW land constrained so solar deployment phased. Initial BESS 50MW for SB-6 compliance = $50M = straightforward compliance play. 4CP payback: $50M / $2.1M/yr = 24 yrs on 4CP alone, but SB-6 uptime insurance + grid reliability = strong case. Source: NREL; CPower 4CP

4_SB6_Economics.curtailment_risk_without_bess: MEDIUM: Bluebonnet EMC must develop curtailment protocols. 1,515 acres = enormous solar potential. Co-op process slower than Oncor but solar+BESS economics strongest in dataset relative to load size.
4_SB6_Economics.sb6_compliance_pathway: BESS 90MW/360MWh for 4CP compliance on 360MW load; solar 1,135MW on 1,515 acres = 315% coverage. GHI 5.30. 4CP savings: 90MW × $42,500 = $3.8M/yr. Solar covers 315% of IT load = significant export potential (or oversizing for future phases).
4_SB6_Economics.notes_economics: 1,135MW BTM solar at $0.95M/MW = $1.078B. BESS 90MW/360MWh at $250/kWh = $90M. Total $1.168B for full solar+BESS. But 360MW initial load = phase in with 100MW solar ($95M) + 45MW BESS ($45M) = $140M initial phase. 4CP payback: 45MW BESS vs $3.8M/yr savings = ~37M investment / $1.9M savings = 19 yrs on 4CP alone. Grid reliability and SB-6 compliance drive the real case. Source: NREL; Bluebonnet EMC territory analysis

5_SB6_Regulatory.applies_to: New or expanded large loads ≥75MW in ERCOT seeking interconnection
5_SB6_Regulatory.effective_date: Sep 1 2025 (SB-6 effective); PUCT rules finalized mid-2026
5_SB6_Regulatory.requirement: Must: (1) pay study fee ≥$100,000; (2) demonstrate site control; (3) disclose duplicate TX interconnection requests; (4) provide financial commitment for transmission upgrades; (5) disclose on-site backup generation if capable of ≥50% of on-site demand
5_SB6_Regulatory.bess_impact: BESS disclosed as 'backup generation' per §39.170; if BESS can serve ≥50% of load = must disclose; ERCOT may direct BESS deployment during emergencies; BESS reduces net grid capacity request = smaller transmission upgrade = lower financial commitment
5_SB6_Regulatory.current_status: ACTIVE — PUCT Project 58481 published draft rule Fall 2025; public comments filed; final rule expected mid-2026. Developers planning around standards NOW per Perkins Coie Jan 2026.
5_SB6_Regulatory.source: SB-6 §39.170; PUCT Project 58481; Perkins Coie Jan 2026; Weil Jul 2025; Bracewell Oct 2025

5_SB6_Regulatory.applies_to: New large loads ≥75MW interconnecting AFTER Dec 31 2025 (excluding critical industrial loads and critical natural gas facilities)
5_SB6_Regulatory.requirement: MANDATORY: Utilities (TSPs, co-ops, MOEs) must develop curtailment protocols. Equipment/technology allowing remote disconnection must be INSTALLED BEFORE energization. ERCOT may order curtailment only during firm load shed. 'Kill switch' required.
5_SB6_Regulatory.bess_impact: DIRECT MANDATE: If BESS can serve ≥50% of load, ERCOT may direct BESS deployment INSTEAD OF curtailment during grid emergencies. BESS therefore serves as curtailment buffer — maintains uptime during SB-6 events. Critical compliance tool for data centers that cannot accept outages.
5_SB6_Regulatory.current_status: EFFECTIVE for new interconnections Jan 1 2026. Utilities must develop protocols. Kill switch installation required before energization. PUCT still finalizing implementation details under Project 58317.
5_SB6_Regulatory.source: SB-6 PURA §39.170(a); Pillsbury Jul 2025; Bracewell Oct 2025; Baker Botts Jul 2025

5_SB6_Regulatory.provision: Net Metering Review for Co-Located Generation
5_SB6_Regulatory.applies_to: New net metering arrangements between EXISTING (as of Sep 1 2025) stand-alone generators and new large load customers ≥75MW
5_SB6_Regulatory.requirement: Generator must submit Notice of Proposed Net Metering Arrangement to ERCOT before implementation. ERCOT has 120 days to study system impacts. PUCT has 60 days to approve/deny/impose conditions. If no PUCT decision in 180 days = deemed approved.
5_SB6_Regulatory.bess_impact: APPLIES to: existing BTM gas generator + new large load = §39.169 review required. DOES NOT APPLY to: new generator + new large load (built together) = clean path. KEY INSIGHT: Building new solar+BESS collocated with new data center (Google Haskell model) AVOIDS §39.169 review entirely.
5_SB6_Regulatory.current_status: ACTIVE — Net Metering Rules (PUCT Project 58479) draft published Fall 2025; public comments filed; adoption targeted early 2026. Existing generators (Stand-Alone Generation Resources as of Sep 1 2025) list published by ERCOT Sep 19 2025.
5_SB6_Regulatory.source: SB-6 PURA §39.169; ERCOT Stand-Alone Gen Resources list Sep 19 2025; Baker Botts Jul 2025; K&L Gates Feb 2025

5_SB6_Regulatory.applies_to: BTM/PUN arrangements where: new/expanded large load + generation that can serve ≥50% of demand + generation does NOT export to grid
5_SB6_Regulatory.mw_threshold: ≥50% of load served by on-site gen that doesn't export
5_SB6_Regulatory.requirement: ERCOT may direct facility to: (1) reduce net consumption OR (2) activate on-site backup generation. ONLY during grid emergencies. ONLY after ERCOT has exhausted ALL available market services. This is the most limited curtailment authority.
5_SB6_Regulatory.bess_impact: BESS that doesn't export = qualifies as 'backup generation' under §37.0561. If BESS serves ≥50% of load, ERCOT can direct BESS discharge during emergencies. This is LESS onerous than §39.170 curtailment — BESS maintains facility operations while grid is protected. Perfect compliance architecture.
5_SB6_Regulatory.current_status: EFFECTIVE. Limited scope — only applies when ERCOT has exhausted market services. In practice: BESS enables data centers to comply with emergency directives without actual downtime. This is the key insight for the demo.
5_SB6_Regulatory.source: SB-6 PURA §37.0561; Baker Botts Jul 2025 analysis; Weil Jul 2025

5_SB6_Regulatory.provision: Voluntary Large Load Demand Management Service
5_SB6_Regulatory.requirement: ERCOT will develop competitive voluntary demand response service. Large loads can voluntarily reduce demand during anticipated emergency conditions. At least 24-hour notice required. Real-time load telemetry and fast-response demand controllers required.
5_SB6_Regulatory.bess_impact: BESS enables participation in voluntary demand management service. BESS discharges on 24-hour notice = facility maintains operations while reducing grid draw = earns revenue from ERCOT demand response market. Turns SB-6 compliance cost into revenue stream.
5_SB6_Regulatory.current_status: RULEMAKING IN PROGRESS — PUCT Project 58482 scoping started Jan 2026; adoption planned late 2026. Early movers who install BESS now will be positioned to participate in voluntary program and earn revenue.
5_SB6_Regulatory.source: SB-6 §39.170; PUCT Project 58482; Perkins Coie Jan 2026; FlexGen analysis Nov 2025

5_SB6_Regulatory.applies_to: All large loads in ERCOT; methodology review
5_SB6_Regulatory.requirement: PUCT must evaluate 4CP methodology to ensure large loads 'appropriately assign costs for transmission investment.' May result in higher transmission charges for large loads at peak periods. Could increase the financial incentive for BESS peak shaving.
5_SB6_Regulatory.bess_impact: IF 4CP methodology changes to allocate more costs to large loads: BESS 4CP avoidance value INCREASES above current $40-45K/MW/yr benchmark. Early BESS deployment locks in current economics before potential cost increases. Urgency argument for acting now.
5_SB6_Regulatory.current_status: UNDER REVIEW — PUCT Project 58484; 90-day evaluation required from SB-6 effective date; rule changes by Dec 31 2026. Current 4CP benchmark: $40,000-45,000/MW/yr (CPower; Ndustrial industry data 2025).
5_SB6_Regulatory.source: SB-6 §39.170 (4CP review); PUCT Project 58484; CPower 4CP benchmark; Ndustrial 2025; Arcus Power summer 2025 analysis

5_SB6_Regulatory.pura_section: Texas Energy Fund legislation (separate from SB-6)
5_SB6_Regulatory.applies_to: 18 new gas projects (>7GW) advancing through ERCOT interconnection
5_SB6_Regulatory.requirement: State-funded gas generation. 18 projects totaling >7GW. All filed Full Interconnection Study (FIS) with ERCOT. First project (Pin Peaking) received Part 1 Approval to Energize Nov 2025. These are the gas plants that will eventually provide baseload — BUT will not serve data centers needing power in 2026-2027.
5_SB6_Regulatory.bess_impact: TEF gas plants are the ALTERNATIVE to BTM solar+BESS for baseload. Timeline: 2026-2029. BTM solar+BESS bridges the 2-3 year gap before TEF gas comes online. After TEF plants arrive, BESS retains value for 4CP management and SB-6 compliance.
5_SB6_Regulatory.current_status: IN PROGRESS — All 18 FIS filed per ERCOT Nov 2025 board update. First energization ~2026-2027. Full 7GW by 2029. Source: ERCOT Dec 2025 board presentation.
5_SB6_Regulatory.source: ERCOT Dec 2025 System Planning Update; Texas Energy Fund legislation; ERCOT board Dec 2025
